facts.text: Irvington’s name came from a 19th‑century railroad move when locals persuaded the Hudson River Railroad to rename the station and post office.
The village’s Main Street side lanes were once labeled A, B, C, etc., before being renamed after early settlers like Dutcher and Buckhout.
The 1881 Burnham factory, rebuilt after a fire, earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places in 1999.
Across the tracks, former Lord & Burnham factory buildings now house about 60 companies, shops, and restaurants as Bridge Street Properties.
The Irvington Historical Society moved into the restored McVickar House in 2005, turning it into the village’s history center.
In April 2021, the Irvington Shakespeare Company opened, performing at the historic Irvington Theater and partnering with the local arts community.
